items.text: Dana Whitfield, product manager at a healthcare data company in Portland
Sam, partner of six years; described as 'great' support during difficult times
Marcus, brother, lives in Denver; they are close but haven't seen each other in over a year, currently only phone contact
Mid-quarter at work with roadmap review on Thursday 2025-02-06; working through prioritization between interoperability work and analytics dashboard for exec team
items.archivedReason: Work roadmap review from 2025-02-06 is stale; nearly three months old with no follow-up

items.text: Wants to see brother Marcus in person this year after over a year of phone-only contact — described as 'a real intention'

supersededBy.text: Upgraded to concrete plan after Nina's birth 2025-03-06; see new thread about visiting before summer 2025 (resolved 2025-03-09)

items.text: Lost mother recently (as of 2025-02-03 session, described as 'heavy winter'); said 'it colors everything'; did not want to discuss details in this session
Priya, closest friend from grad school; ceramicist and freelance designer; they signed up for a Tuesday night pottery class together starting 2025-02-12
Started attending Tuesday night pottery class with friend Priya (as of 2025-02-12); first standing regular activity they've had together in years

Mother had a 22-foot wooden sailboat called the Dorothy Jean, named after Dana's grandmother. Mom taught Dana to sail on that boat. The boat has been under a tarp in Priya's dad's barn near Scappoose since mom got sick. On Sunday 2025-02-09, Dana pulled the tarp off and sanded one plank — the first step in deciding to restore the boat slowly, with no deadline. Dana said working with hands on something of mom's 'felt like the first right thing in months.' (2025-02-12)
Restoring mother's sailboat Dorothy Jean at barn near Scappoose; working Sundays, slowly, no deadline (started 2025-02-09)

Training for Lakefront Marathon October 2025; signed up 2025-02-24. Running four times weekly, long run Saturdays (eight miles 2025-02-22 along river). Casual runner for years (5Ks, one half marathon ~four years ago). Chose marathon as 'something with a long arc' during difficult winter.
items.compressedFrom: Training for Lakefront Marathon in October 2025; signed up 2025-02-24. Running four times per week with long run every Saturday morning. Did eight miles on Saturday 2025-02-22 along the river. Has been a casual runner for years (5Ks, one half marathon about four years ago). Chose marathon as 'something with a long arc' that's just theirs during difficult winter.

items.text: On 2025-02-24, Dana said of signing up for the marathon: 'It's the first time since mom that I've been excited about something in the future instead of dreading it.'
Elena: Marcus's partner; gave birth to daughter Nina 2025-03-06
items.compressedFrom: Elena: Elena, Marcus's partner; gave birth to daughter Nina on 2025-03-06 (as of 2025-03-09)

items.text: Nina: niece, daughter of Marcus and Elena; born 2025-03-06. Marcus called Dana from hospital ~2am to share news. Dana 'cried at my desk' seeing first photo of Nina in knit hat.
items.compressedFrom: Nina: Nina, niece, daughter of Marcus and Elena; born 2025-03-06. Marcus called Dana from hospital at ~2am to share the news. Dana said 'I cried at my desk' seeing first photo of Nina in a knit hat. (as of 2025-03-09)

items.text: On 2025-03-09, Dana learned Marcus and Elena had their daughter Nina on 2025-03-06. Marcus called from the hospital hallway at ~2am 'just to say "she's here."' Dana said: 'We haven't talked like that — voice shaking, no agenda — in years. Denver suddenly feels way too far away.' (2025-03-09)

Wants to visit Marcus, Elena, and baby Nina in Denver before summer 2025 'while she's still tiny'; Marcus said 'the guest room is yours.' Dana described this as 'a thing I owe now, and I want to owe it' — 'the good kind' of obligation. (opened 2025-03-09)
[as of 2025-03-09] Marathon training on schedule; ran ten miles on Saturday 2025-03-07 in light snow flurries
[as of 2025-03-09] Dorothy Jean restoration progressing; port side fully sanded as of 2025-03-09

supersededBy.text: Dorothy Jean restoration progressing; starboard side sanded, started re-caulking hull seams (as of 2025-05-11)

items.text: On March 14, 2025, Dana scattered Mom's ashes at Beacon Rock in the Columbia River Gorge. Dana went alone. Beacon Rock was 'ours' — Mom used to take Dana there as a kid, and on the way up taught Dana to read cloud shapes: 'Not just "that one's a rabbit" — she had this whole taxonomy, what a mare's tail sky meant, when the anvil clouds meant get off the water.' Dana said: 'I stood at the top and the sky was doing exactly the kind of high thin herringbone she loved, and I let her go into the wind.' The urn had been in Dana's closet for months; Dana 'couldn't even look at that side of the closet.' Dana picked up a fir cone from the trail on the way down; it's on the windowsill above the kitchen sink now. Sam had soup waiting when Dana got home. Dana slept eleven hours. (2025-03-18)
[as of 2025-03-18] In a cutback week in marathon training (as of 2025-03-18)

Sam received job offer as Perception team lead at robotics startup in Austin, Texas (as of 2025-04-01); strong offer in his dream problem space. Decision deadline early May 2025. Sam said he won't take it if it costs Dana this year of healing. Both weighing carefully, not fighting.
items.compressedFrom: Sam received job offer as Perception team lead at robotics startup in Austin, Texas (as of 2025-04-01); offer is strong and represents his dream problem space — he's wanted to return to robotics for years. Decision deadline early May 2025. Sam said he won't take it if it costs Dana this year of healing. Dana and Sam are not fighting; both weighing the decision carefully.

items.text: Deciding whether to move to Austin for Sam's job vs. staying in Portland. Could find PM work in Austin, but 'whole life fabric is here' — Priya, pottery, barn, boat, geography ('Mom's here... the Gorge, Beacon Rock, the barn. This geography is load-bearing for me right now'). Decision needed early May 2025.
items.compressedFrom: Deciding whether to move to Austin for Sam's job offer vs. staying in Portland. Dana feels torn: could find PM work in Austin, but 'whole life fabric is here' — Priya, pottery class, the barn, the boat restoration, and the geography itself ('Mom's here... the Gorge, Beacon Rock, the barn. This geography is load-bearing for me right now'). Decision needed by early May 2025. (opened 2025-04-01)

supersededBy.text: Sam accepted Austin robotics startup job with remote-from-Portland arrangement negotiated; starts June 2025, will work from spare room with week in Austin every couple months. Startup agreed to counter-offer. Dana and Sam staying in Portland. Decision made early May 2025 without conflict. Dana described relief at not having to leave 'load-bearing geography' this year.

items.text: Marathon training week six as of 2025-04-01; ran twelve miles on Saturday 2025-03-29 with no complaints

supersededBy.text: Withdrew from Lakefront Marathon October 2025 on 2025-04-14 after ankle sprain; cannot safely complete training with eight-to-ten week recovery window. Withdrew registration immediately to avoid 'waffling.'

items.text: Sprained ankle badly on Saturday 2025-04-13 during trail run in Forest Park (mile nine, wet root); grade-two sprain or worse per urgent care. In walking boot; seeing orthopedist Thursday 2025-04-18. Doctor said eight to ten weeks before running possible again.

Withdrew from Lakefront Marathon October 2025 on 2025-04-14 after ankle sprain; cannot safely complete training with eight-to-ten week recovery window. Withdrew registration immediately to avoid 'waffling.'

On 2025-04-14, after withdrawing from the marathon, Dana said: 'That training arc was the first future-shaped thing I'd built since mom.' Sam pointed out the fitness isn't lost and there's always next year; Dana said 'he's right, and I don't want to hear it yet.'
Ankle healing on schedule per orthopedist; walking boot comes off Thursday 2025-05-01, physical therapy to start after (as of 2025-04-27)

supersededBy.text: Ankle boot removed; walking normally-ish; attending physical therapy twice weekly (as of 2025-05-11)

items.text: Sam's decision deadline on Austin job offer is one week from 2025-04-27 (early May 2025 as previously stated)

supersededBy.text: Sam accepted Austin robotics startup Perception team lead role with remote-from-Portland arrangement; starts June 2025, working from spare room with week in Austin every couple months (as of 2025-05-11)

items.text: Rift with Priya after Dana declined to lend her $8,000 for ceramics studio lease deposit and buildout (as of ~2025-04-13 to 2025-04-15). Dana said no immediately and 'badly,' citing uncertain finances with Austin decision pending and past experience lending money to an ex that 'poisoned everything.' Priya went 'very quiet and then very polite.' They have skipped pottery class twice in the nearly two weeks since. Dana described it as delivering 'ten years of friendship a verdict in four seconds.' Dana is 'pretty sure' she won't change the answer but regrets how she delivered it. Described as 'too raw' to repair yet as of 2025-04-27. (opened 2025-04-27)

Sam accepted Austin robotics startup Perception team lead role with remote-from-Portland arrangement; starts June 2025, working from spare room with week in Austin every couple months (as of 2025-05-11)
Ankle boot removed; walking normally-ish; attending physical therapy twice weekly (as of 2025-05-11)
Dorothy Jean restoration progressing; starboard side sanded, started re-caulking hull seams (as of 2025-05-11)

On 2025-05-11, after Sam negotiated remote arrangement for Austin job and they decided to stay in Portland, Dana said: 'When he told me they'd agreed, I cried a little, which surprised me. I hadn't let myself feel how scared I was of leaving this year — the barn, the boat, Beacon Rock, all of it. And I'd been quietly rehearsing how to be graceful about going. Not needing the rehearsal anymore was the relief.' (2025-05-11)

entries.note: Serious conflict as of mid-April 2025 over loan request; relationship currently strained with communication paused. See open thread.
